headline: South Florida's Economic Engine Revs Up: Consumer Confidence Rises, World Cup Fever Builds, But Spring Break Takes a Hit

stories.title: FIFA 2026 World Cup Setting South Florida Up for Major Economic Windfall
stories.summary: <cite index="11-1,11-2">The 2026 FIFA World Cup is coming to South Florida with matches at Hard Rock Stadium, only 100 days away and poised to bring a major surge in customers.</cite> <cite index="11-24,11-25">Officials anticipate about a million folks generating $1.3 billion in economic impact for Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach counties.</cite>
stories.takeaway: Lowkey this is huge for the region—tourism, hospitality, and local businesses are prepping for the biggest economic boost in years, but logistics and infrastructure are the real challenge.

stories.title: Florida Consumer Sentiment Hits Three-Month High, Shaping Major Buying Decisions
stories.summary: <cite index="8-1">Consumer sentiment in Florida rose for a third straight month, reflecting stronger financial outlooks that can shape major buying decisions.</cite> This momentum signals renewed optimism across the state's economy.
stories.takeaway: Tbh this is lowkey encouraging—people are feeling better about their finances and ready to spend, which is great for retail and real estate sectors.

stories.title: Fort Lauderdale Spring Break Slowing Down Due to Alcohol Ordinance
stories.summary: <cite index="12-2,12-3,12-4">Spring break business has slowed on Fort Lauderdale Beach, with local businesses citing a new city ordinance banning outdoor alcohol sales and open containers, with some operators noting about 30 percent of sales typically come from to-go drinks.</cite>
stories.takeaway: This ordinance is literally cutting into beach bar profits—some owners are seeing massive revenue drops compared to normal years, though they're hopeful business picks up later in the month.

stories.title: Florida Economy Set to Outperform Nation Again in 2026 Despite Moderation
stories.summary: <cite index="2-1,2-2">Florida's economic growth has outpaced the nation by an average of 2.3 percentage points over the past four years and is expected to exceed national GDP growth again in 2026, though by a narrower margin than recent years.</cite> <cite index="2-11">Florida's unemployment rate is projected to range between 4.2 and 4.3 percent in 2026.</cite>
stories.takeaway: Fr the state's still crushing it economically—slower growth than before but stable and solid, which is actually healthy long-term.

watchNext.title: Miami Tech Hub Emerging as Gateway to Latin America—Companies Relocating in 2026
watchNext.why: <cite index="18-1,18-2">Miami has rapidly become a magnet for startups and tech ventures, with its strategic location as a gateway to Latin America, diverse talent pool, and growing tech ecosystem positioning it uniquely for regional expansion.</cite>

watchNext.title: D-Wave Quantum Computing Headquarters Relocates to Boca Raton—FAU Gets $20M Quantum Computer
watchNext.why: <cite index="22-11,22-12">Palm Beach County got a sizable lift when D-Wave announced relocating its headquarters from California to Boca Raton, while Florida Atlantic University purchased a $20 million quantum computer, making it the state's first and only university to host an onsite quantum computer.</cite>
